symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden violent coughing fit during a meal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A protective cough triggered by a bolus breaching the laryngeal inlet during eating.
Choking during eating can present identically at onset; distinguished by whether the cough and voice are preserved.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transient facial cyanosis during the episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Documents a hypoxic event and marks the episode as severe/airway-threatening.

symptoms.symptom_name: Persisting wet/congested cough and tachypnoea after the acute event

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Large-airway irritation and secretions after aspiration.
Non-specific early; the impending complication to watch for over 24-48h.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved ability to cough and phonate; no visible oral obstruction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A patient who can cough and vocalise does not have complete airway obstruction; back-blows/thrusts are not indicated.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic severe oropharyngeal dysphagia on a modified diet

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The mechanistic substrate — an impaired protective swallow makes aspiration the leading explanation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Residual right hemiparesis and expressive aphasia unchanged from baseline

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deficits at chronic baseline reduce the probability of a new stroke, but a telehealth history cannot fully exclude one.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oxygen saturation 94% on a facility fingertip oximeter

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Borderline/mildly low; patient-obtainable and admissible in telehealth but unverified and not reassuring on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Coarse transmitted upper-airway rhonchi bilaterally with no focal basal crackles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transmitted large-airway sounds fit aspiration/secretions; absence of focal crackles argues established pneumonia has not yet set in — a clinician auscultation finding the AI cannot obtain.
No focal crackles at this moment, but sensitivity is limited and pneumonia can evolve over 24-48h.
Impaired airway protection consistent with post-stroke dysphagia; a bedside clinician finding, not obtainable by chat.
Confirms the chronic structural basis of the deficits and dysphagia; historical imaging, not a live telehealth finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest X-ray for evolving aspiration pneumonia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consolidation (classically dependent segments) would confirm aspiration pneumonia; unavailable in telehealth, needs in-person imaging.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute aspiration event secondary to post-stroke dysphagia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Coughing fit plus transient cyanosis occurring during a meal in a patient with known severe dysphagia is a near-specific picture of an aspiration event.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person chest auscultation and airway/respiratory assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The chest cannot be auscultated remotely; an urgent hands-on respiratory assessment is needed to gauge ongoing aspiration and airway safety.
To detect evolving aspiration pneumonia/pneumonitis over the next 24-48 hours if respiratory signs persist or a fever develops.
To reassess diet texture and swallowing safety before any further oral intake; the mechanism will recur otherwise.
Not indicated while the patient can cough and phonate (airway patent); reserved for complete obstruction with an ineffective cough.
